expected_contracts: normalized direction propagation through telemetry and persistence

feedback_files.tests/test_feedback.py:
from trading.paper_trading import PaperBook, auto_enter_from_signals
from trading.portfolio_risk import RiskLimits, size_position


def test_short_position_sizing_uses_stop_above_entry():
    assert size_position(10_000.0, 100.0, 105.0, limits=RiskLimits(), direction="short") == 20
    assert size_position(10_000.0, 100.0, 95.0, limits=RiskLimits(), direction="short") == 0


def test_short_signal_preserves_direction_across_entry_and_telemetry():
    book = PaperBook()
    entered = auto_enter_from_signals(
        book,
        [{
            "ticker": "MRVL",
            "entry_price": 300.29,
            "stop_price": 337.15,
            "target_price": 291.70,
            "confidence": 0.89,
            "direction": "short",
        }],
        10_000.0,
    )
    assert entered == 1
    assert len(book.trades) == 1
    assert book.trades[0].direction == "short"
    assert book.trades[0].stop_price > book.trades[0].entry_price
    assert book.trades[0].target_price < book.trades[0].entry_price
    assert book.trades[0].quantity > 0
    assert book.emitted[0].direction == "short"
